name: Rajesh Dammala
education: Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science Engineering - JNTU, Hyderabad (2008)

sharedMetrics: 16+ years across cloud operations, production engineering, escalation leadership, and data center environments
20+ mission-critical production services supported in 24x7x365 environments
30-35% incident automation potential delivered through AI-driven workflows
175+ troubleshooting guides and operational playbooks authored in 5 months
32% faster issue resolution through escalation framework redesign
Teams led across global geographies: up to 32 engineers
200+ bugs and feature enhancements raised across Azure operations, ICM, JIT, and Key Vault onboarding
~122K USD estimated annual savings through AI copilots and automation initiatives
150+ engineering hours per year saved through automation and onboarding tooling

experience.baseTitle: Lead Escalations Manager / SRE Program Lead
highlights: Serve as central escalation authority for high-severity incidents across Xbox support services and adjacent production platforms.
Architected Azure SRE Agent platform for AI-driven incident diagnostics, triage, and remediation workflows across non-production and production environments.
Designed automation integrating ICM, Application Insights, Log Analytics, Azure DevOps, Kusto, SQL Server, Cosmos DB, and Microsoft Teams notifications.
Drove operational efficiency from 85% to 98% through alert rationalization, stale incident cleanup, playbook standardization, and automation-driven triage.
Remediated 25,000+ stale or false incidents and streamlined 500+ alerts to improve signal quality for engineering and operations teams.
Authored 175+ troubleshooting guides and operational playbooks in 5 months, accelerating onboarding and reducing repeat investigation effort.
Built Power BI and KQL dashboards for telemetry, service health, and LiveSite trends used in executive reviews and operational decision-making.
Led AI enablement initiatives including ADO Bug Intelligence, RF Insights Copilot, CAD Support Copilot, and prompt library efforts.
Managed production Azure DevOps build and release pipelines for Xbox support services, with emphasis on release quality, reliability, and controlled change.
Supported 24x7x365 mission-critical systems and participated directly in on-call and incident command workflows.
Led a team of 15 engineers supporting Azure customer workloads, enterprise subscriptions, capacity questions, data migrations, and cross-team technical issues.
Spearheaded Azure chat support initiatives and improved service responsiveness through process and tooling improvements.
Managed Azure subscription and enterprise agreement scenarios, region capacity questions, identity concerns, and migration issues for customers and internal teams.
Partnered with Microsoft product and engineering groups to escalate customer-impacting issues and advocate for product enhancements.
Drove support quality and operational maturity in high-volume cloud environments requiring technical depth and stakeholder clarity.
Led an operations organization spanning 12 senior engineers and 20 service engineers across Hyderabad and Redmond.
Performed deep technical investigations into complex Azure production issues from cluster to blade level.
Created PowerShell automation that saved thousands of manual hours annually across investigations, onboarding, and operational workflows.
Drove Azure network monitoring, change management, incident handling, and large-scale service health investigations.
Raised 200+ bugs and feature requests tied to ICM, JIT, Azure Key Vault onboarding, and operational platform improvements.

experience.baseTitle: Systems Analyst / Support Engineer / Data Center Engineer
highlights: Built and decommissioned enterprise servers, configured clusters, patched systems, and supported production data center operations.
Migrated large data volumes and installed packaged tools and applications across server environments.
Supported foundational infrastructure engineering practices including hardware lifecycle, environment readiness, and deployment execution.
